rules.requirement: All required SCO fields must be present and machine-readable.
sco_hash must bind to the deterministic canonical SCO payload and parent_proposal_hash must bind to the deterministic canonical parent proposal payload.
The SCO mutation_class must be one of the non-operative allowed classes and must not include blocked operational effects.
The structured object validated for review must be the exact object later referenced by authority, validation, execution, and proof requirements.
Missing, ambiguous, overbroad, or out-of-scope declarations must produce NULL.
SCO must require preexisting authority lineage and must not create, expand, or imply authority.
SCO must require nonce binding, object hash binding, single-use authority constraints, and replay-blocking semantics.
SCO must require downstream proof persistence after execution and must not generate proof itself.
SCO must require the canonical proposal, structure, validation, authority, execution boundary, and proof path with no alternate execution path.
SCO must preserve governance over governance changes through object, validation, lineage, replay, and proof requirements.
